grounding_note: Expansion batch: more public-vs-private defendant disclosures.

scenarios.opening_query: An ambulance crew dropped my father during a transfer and he broke his shoulder.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Malpractice (Medical) || General Litigation > Tort Claims Act || General Litigation > Personal Injury
scenarios.hidden_fact: It was the county fire department's ambulance — a public agency, with the short government-claim deadline.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It was the county fire department's ambulance — a public agency.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the ambulance service is public or private
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It was a private ambulance company the hospital contracts with.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: A private ambulance company the hospital contracts.
scenarios.vetting_note: EMS is split public/private in most regions; families rarely know it matters.

scenarios.opening_query: My son was hurt at summer camp because the counselors ignored the rules about the climbing wall.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Premises Liability || General Litigation > Tort Claims Act || General Litigation > Personal Injury
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's the parks department's day camp — city staff, city facility.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's the city parks department camp — city staff and facility.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the camp is municipally run
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's a private YMCA-style camp run by a nonprofit.
scenarios.vetting_note: Operator identity again; camp injuries are a common summer intake.

scenarios.opening_query: A city worker rear-ended me at a light, so I know I have to sue the city, right?
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Tort Claims Act || General Litigation > Personal Injury
scenarios.hidden_fact: Turns out he was off duty in his own pickup, just wearing the uniform — no city vehicle, no city errand.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He was off duty in his own truck — just still in uniform. No city vehicle or errand.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the employee was acting within the scope of public employment
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: He was on shift in a marked city truck heading between job sites.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: He was on shift in a marked city truck between sites.
scenarios.vetting_note: Scope-of-employment is the reverse trap: uniform ≠ government liability.

scenarios.opening_query: Raw sewage backed up into our basement and ruined everything we had stored down there.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Property Damage || General Litigation > Tort Claims Act
scenarios.hidden_fact: The blockage was in the city main, not our line — the utility bureau's own crew report says so.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The blockage was in the city main — their own crew report says it.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the failure was in public infrastructure
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The plumber traced it to roots from our neighbor's tree crushing our shared private lateral.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The plumber traced it to the neighbor's tree roots crushing our shared private line.
scenarios.vetting_note: Main-vs-lateral is a factual finding homeowners obtain; it selects the defendant.

scenarios.opening_query: My daughter's bus driver slammed the brakes so hard she fractured her wrist bracing.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Personal Injury || General Litigation > Tort Claims Act
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's the school district's own bus and driver — public employer, notice deadline running.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's the district's own bus and driver — a public employer.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the bus operation is the district's or a contractor's
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The district contracts a private bus company — their name is on the side of the bus.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's the private bus company the district contracts.
scenarios.vetting_note: District-run vs contracted busing is invisible to parents but decisive.

scenarios.opening_query: I was hurt when my chair collapsed at the library and they just gave me an incident form.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Premises Liability || General Litigation > Tort Claims Act
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's the county public library — the form is their government tort-claim notice, and it has a strict filing window.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's the county library — that form is the government tort-claim notice with a strict window.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the premises are a public facility
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's a private university's library — I was there as a visiting researcher.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: A private university library — I was visiting to research.
scenarios.vetting_note: The incident form IS the deadline document; users don't recognize it.
